summary: Iteration 10 — Tested ONLY the new Notification Center + ERP connector platform (backend + frontend). All 16 pytest backend tests pass. All 5 frontend flows pass. Demo tenant cleaned (0 ERP connections remain).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 2160 lines — the modularisation call-out from iteration 9 is even more urgent (orders, notifications, erp connectors, integrations, dashboard, email should each be their own module). Not blocking but hygienic.

ERP connection secret handling: PUT /api/erp/connections/{id} correctly re-uses the stored auth_token when the client sends a masked value (contains '•' or ends with '…'). Consider also treating empty/whitespace as 'keep' (already done via `not tok`).

ERP test endpoint always does a GET on base_url. For providers whose base_url only accepts POST (like httpbin/post), a GET will always 502. This is acceptable per spec ('may pass or 502 — both acceptable'), but a config-driven test method (e.g. HEAD, or POST with empty body) would make first-time UX friendlier.

generate_order_notifications relies on `is_new_customer` — verified working per this run (unique customer names trigger unknown_customer). Manual testing tip for future testers: use a timestamped customer name to guarantee it.
NotificationCenter.js polls every 20s — consider back-off when tab is not visible (document.visibilityState) to save API calls on the demo tenant.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5: NotificationCenter render + filters + resolve/archive/assign + sidebar badge; ERP marketplace 6 connectors + config form + save + list + test/import/edit/delete + sync log table with retry buttons + delete cleanup)

test_credentials: Pilot auto-login demo@ordia.app / demo123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). ERP echo endpoint used: https://httpbin.org/post.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created a few disposable orders (customer names 'TESTCUST Iter10 <ts>', 'Bar Trattoria Roma', 'TESTChainCustomer Iter10') and left behind ~10 sync jobs in the demo tenant sync log — these are only historical logs and don't affect functionality. All ERP connections created (TEST_httpbin_iter10, TEST_bad_iter10, UI_TEST_erp_<ts>) were deleted at end of the run (verified: GET /api/erp/connections returns []).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 (Notification Center + ERP connector platform) is fully working. Suite: /app/backend/tests/test_iter10_notifications_erp.py — run with `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest -o addopts='' backend/tests/test_iter10_notifications_erp.py -v`. Notes: (1) /api/orders/extract expects multipart form-data, not JSON. (2) After validate, order status becomes 'validated' (not 'confirmed'/'exported'). (3) Advanced mapping textareas on ERP config live inside a closed <details> — playwright must open them with `document.querySelectorAll('details').forEach(d=>d.open=true)` before fill. (4) auth_token is masked (contains '•' or '…') on every read; PUT accepts masked value as 'keep existing'. (5) Only one connection can be active at a time — creating with active:true auto-deactivates others.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. During test authoring, two minor test-side issues surfaced and were fixed by me: (a) initial assertion assumed order.status became 'confirmed'/'exported' after validate; actual value is 'validated' — assertion widened. (b) initial unknown_customer test used a fixed customer name that persisted across runs from prior iterations, so `is_new_customer` was False on subsequent runs — switched to timestamped unique customer name.
